allies.rationale_text: Margin-of-safety complements redundancy by turning spare capacity and backup paths into practical resilience.
allies.source_quote: Systemic and Strategic Redundancy ensures that if one path, asset, or plan fails, others can take over.

antagonists.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory opposes redundancy when duplicated information raises working-memory burden instead of improving learning.
antagonists.source_quote: Redundancy directly conflicts with the goal of reducing Cognitive Load.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Redundancy conflicts with simplification when building redundant safeguards adds system complexity that itself becomes a new failure source.
structured_tensions.source_quote: redundancy conflicts with simplification (simplification) when building redundant safeguards adds system complexity that itself becomes a new failure source.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Redundancy conflicts with optimization-theory when redundancy preserves slack that optimization would eliminate as waste.
structured_tensions.source_quote: redundancy conflicts with optimization-theory (optimization theory) when redundancy preserves slack that optimization would eliminate as waste.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
